author: assistant
conditions: Implementation must remain confined to scripts/build_context_pack.py and its focused tests unless a directly necessary documentation or self-model record change is separately recorded.
Graph-neighborhood behavior must remain disabled by default.
An explicit target artifact ID is required whenever the graph-neighborhood path is enabled.
Maximum depth and graph token budget must reject invalid values and must not take effect unless graph-neighborhood candidate inclusion is enabled.
The first slice must use existing artifact-lineage relations and must not add external edge-record or future-edge-seed input.
Do not modify ask_provider.py, compare_providers.py, prompt_context.py, provider prompts, or prompt rendering.
Do not enable automatic target inference or automatic graph-local context inclusion.
Do not persist graph state or introduce retrieval, embeddings, reranking, production index mutation, memory-store mutation, or runtime-manifest mutation.
GAP-0002 must remain open after this slice.
reason: PLAN-20260716-0001 is a bounded and testable exposure slice for the already implemented CAP-0009 library capability. Repository artifact-lineage relations provide a usable authoritative first input source, so the standalone context-pack CLI can expose explicit target, depth, and graph token-budget controls without introducing an edge-file contract. The plan preserves default behavior, excludes provider entry points, and keeps GAP-0002 open.
scope: Admit only the default-off scripts/build_context_pack.py exposure described by PLAN-20260716-0001: an explicit enable flag, required target artifact ID, optional maximum depth, optional graph-neighborhood token budget, argument dependency validation, forwarding to build_latest_context_manifest, and focused CLI tests. Use existing discovered artifact records and authoritative artifact-lineage relations only.
